description: You are a cloud architect reviewing a legacy deployment. In /home/user/images_to_migrate.log you already have a plain-text list of Docker/OCI image tags, one tag per line. Your goal is to determine how many times each distinct image appears in that list and save the result to an output file. 1. Read the source file located at /home/user/images_to_migrate.log. 2. Produce a frequency report in a **new** file named /home/user/image_frequency.report. 3. The report must fulfil **all** of the following formatting rules so that automated grading can parse it precisely: • Each line describes one unique image tag. • The line must start with the absolute decimal occurrence count, followed by a single space, followed by the exact image tag text. • No leading or trailing whitespace is permitted on any line. • Lines must be ordered by descending count (largest count first). When two images have the same count, order them in ascending (A→Z) lexicographic order of the tag string. • Example of the required line shape (do **not** copy these numbers; they are illustrative only): 42 myorg/service:1.2.3 4. Do not modify the original /home/user/images_to_migrate.log file. 5. When you are done, you can inspect the contents of /home/user/image_frequency.report in the terminal so the grader can capture the final state, but make sure the file itself exists with the required content and exact filename. • A straightforward pipeline combining the coreutils programs sort and uniq (with the -c option) will meet the counting requirement. • Remember to pipe the results through another sort to achieve the final numeric-descending order. • Redirect the final output into /home/user/image_frequency.report.
truth:
<!--
        Ground-truth for automated testing.
        The file /home/user/images_to_migrate.log is pre-seeded with the following 11 lines (final newline included):
    -->
    <initial_file path="/home/user/images_to_migrate.log"><![CDATA[
nginx:1.19
redis:6
nginx:1.19
mongo:4.4
nginx:1.19
redis:6
postgres:12
mongo:4.4
redis:6
redis:6
postgres:12
]]></initial_file>

    <!--
        After the agent completes the task, the grader will look for this exact file and content:
    -->
    <expected_file path="/home/user/image_frequency.report"><![CDATA[
4 redis:6
3 nginx:1.19
2 mongo:4.4
2 postgres:12
]]></expected_file>
name: task_000000_3b9cda92
